title: FFI marshalling ergonomics: string-byte-length builtin + FFI gotchas documentation
goal: Noesis field report: (1) no way to get a string's BYTE length distinct from string-length's codepoint count, so (fwrite s 1 (string-length s) f) silently truncates any string containing multibyte UTF-8 (an em-dash is 3 bytes / 1 char) -- bit lib/core/memory_store.esk's durable append-only log for real. (2) several FFI marshalling conventions were discovered by trial and error and needed writing down: C NULL arrives as the empty list (not 0/#f); a returned ptr/char* is always an opaque handle requiring explicit (ptr->string p)  (a field report claimed this differs between interpreter and compiled runtime for getenv specifically -- did not reproduce in isolation on current HEAD, documented as such); variadic extern declarations corrupt arguments past the fixed prefix on arm64.
root_cause: string-length always counted codepoints (via eshkol_utf8_strlen); there was no codegen builtin exposing the string header's raw byte-size field, even though the runtime already had eshkol_string_byte_length (lib/core/runtime_string.cpp) fully implemented and JIT-registered but never wired to any Scheme-callable name. Separately, several FFI marshalling behaviors (NULL->(), opaque ptr returns, unsafe variadic externs) were tribal knowledge rather than documented, and variadic externs had no warning at the point a user declares one.
fix: Added `string-byte-length` as a new codegen builtin mirroring string-length's dispatch: StringIOCodegen::stringByteLength (string_io_codegen.h/.cpp) calls the pre-existing eshkol_string_byte_length runtime function directly (no UTF-8 decoding, just header.size - 1); wired into llvm_codegen.cpp's call-op dispatch, type_checker.cpp's string-length type-narrowing condition (return type Int64), and the REPL/LSP builtin-name lists (repl_utils.h, eshkol_lsp.cpp) for tab-completion parity. Fixed the actual production bug this exists for: lib/core/memory_store.esk's ms-append-fsync! now uses string-byte-length for fwrite's count instead of string-length. Added tests/ffi/string_byte_length_test.esk (ASCII, empty-string, the em-dash case explicitly called out in the bug report -- char-length 1 vs byte-length 3 -- and an fwrite regression check comparing on-disk byte count) plus a new scripts/run_ffi_tests.sh suite (registered in run_all_tests.sh) running it under both -r and AOT. Documented all of item 4's marshalling gotchas in docs/reference/agent/ffi.md (new 'FFI marshalling gotchas' section: byte-vs-codepoint length, NULL->(), opaque ptr returns + getenv field-report note, variadic-extern arm64 corruption) and added a string-byte-length entry to docs/API_REFERENCE.md. Added a compile-time WARNING in llvm_codegen.cpp's codegenExtern when a variadic ('...') extern is declared, pointing at the arm64 corruption risk and the FFI doc section, so the risk surfaces at the declaration site rather than via corrupted runtime data.
acceptance: fwrite with (string-byte-length s) writes the full multibyte string to disk; the on-disk byte count matches
Both -r (JIT) and AOT-compiled binaries agree on all of the above
lib/core/memory_store.esk's append path no longer truncates multibyte UTF-8 log lines
Declaring (extern void f char* ...) emits a WARNING referencing the arm64 variadic-corruption risk and the FFI doc
docs/reference/agent/ffi.md documents: string-byte-length vs string-length, NULL->(), opaque ptr returns, variadic-extern risk
